sourceMovie.title: Striptease
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Striptease (1996) stuck because it commits to its comedy premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Andrew Bergman's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The comedy and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Hustlers
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Striptease clicked for you, Hustlers (2019) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and crime, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Striptease?
faq.answer: Try Hustlers, Matchstick Men, The Old Man & the Gun—each shares DNA with Striptease while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Striptease?
faq.answer: Striptease (1996) is best described as Comedy, Crime, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Striptease worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
